title: Research Program Manager (Research Infrastructure / Model Development / Model Evals & Safety)
location: NYC or SF (primary); London listed as secondary location. Visa sponsorship + relocation offered.
reposted_note: Indeed re-indexed on 2026-06-09 (jobspy date_posted), but Ashby publishedAt is 2026-04-30. True age ~41 days, not 1 day.
biggest_lever: Frontier-model lab (DeepMind/OpenAI/Anthropic alumni) wants 'high-leverage operators, not project trackers, force multipliers who bring clarity to ambiguity' — directly echoes Ryan's founder-associate spike (turning ambiguous founder priorities into workstreams, cross-functional execution at Minima 1->30). Elite company quality (C=90).
biggest_risk: Two compounding gates: (1) PRIMARY location is NYC/SF — London is only a secondary location, and Ryan is UK-only auth, so a London-based applicant is likely deprioritized despite visa-sponsorship language. (2) The role is technical-research program management embedded in frontier-scale ML training infra (Megatron, cluster reliability, pre/mid/post-training runs, blameless post-mortems on training stack) — Ryan has zero ML-infra background and the work indexes on research-process rigor (his stated weakness) over community/GTM (his strength). callback_op 55 < 60 threshold.
apply_move: Only worth a shot if applying explicitly as London-based and reframing for the least-infra variant ('Model Development' over 'Research Infrastructure'). Lead with the 0->1 ambiguity-taming + cross-functional-execution spike, NOT the community/GTM metrics. Honest call: below the 60% bar for Ryan; include for parent visibility, not as a priority application.
